parsed_review.summary: CP00-690 is a descriptor-only RP22 External Integrations I P08/P09 review-handoff foundation bridge pack (150 units, RP22.P08.M06.S09-RP22.P09.M03.S22, risk C). The closeout evidence is internally consistent and the pack-level claims are supported. The pack correctly claims production_ready=false / runtime_ready=false and is in pending_review state awaiting exactly one hardened read-only Claude review (this review). VERIFIED BY READ-ONLY INSPECTION: (1) registry.js:1411-1657 CP690 binding+requirements match the baseline exactly — pack_id CP00-690, risk C, 150 units, range RP22.P08.M06.S09-RP22.P09.M03.S22, upstream CP00-689, next CP00-691/RP22.P09.M04.S01; deliverable counts {hermes_evidence:42, claude_review:15, implementation:73, test:8, security_audit:8, ui:4}=150, phase {P08:88,P09:62}=150, and micro-phase/micro-title counts each sum to 150. (2) service.js:3369-3415 descriptor/packet/handoff factories are descriptor-only with every row descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false, customer_safe_errors_only=true, raw_payload_included=false; freezeCp690Result (585-592) stamps full no-write/no-real-data attestation; authority_boundary keeps claude_is_final_approval=false and human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true; Hermes packet hermes_runtime_opened=false and runtime_receipt_emitted=false (Hermes used only for deterministic control-plane packet/receipt handling, not product runtime). (3) validators.js:1023-1041 and 2117-2130 enforce coverage distributions and assert all descriptor-only flags true and all runtime/permission/audit/credential/persistence/command flags false, plus claude-not-final. (4) model.test.js:646-657 and 1241-1274 assert coverage and runtime-closed boundaries, handoff to CP00-691/RP22.P09.M04.S01, and contract projection current_pack=CP00-690 with historical_pack_ids through CP00-689. (5) index.js re-exports via export *; validate script (validate-rp22-external-integrations-i-contract.mjs) weaves CP690 through the binding/coverage/descriptor/hermes/claude/handoff/projection chain. (6) Evidence files agree: manifest descriptor-only/deviation_from_plan=false with both 150-unit lists ending at RP22.P09.M03.S22; command-evidence writes_product_state=false/no_real_data=true; construction-inspection and adjudication consistently pending review. REVIEW HARDENING: readiness auth preflight passed (exit 0, valid JSON result, 1455-byte stdout, no stderr); validation-report confirms prompt carries no full diff/source (read_tools_used), summary-only prohibited for risk C, schema present, no forbidden tools; review-baseline contains no secrets/credentials/raw payloads. AUTHORITY BOUNDARY preserved: Claude remains read-only non-final reviewer; enterprise-trust closed outside this receipt; pack does not claim runtime or enterprise trust, so blocks_goal_closeout=false. PROVENANCE CAVEAT: the 22 pre-review validation commands are asserted exit 0 in command-evidence and are consistent with the inspected source, but were not independently re-executed in this read-only review. One non-blocking P3 transparency note on the coverage-validation topology is recorded below.

findings.title: Live-pack coverage resolved via manifest plan_binding_snapshot rather than an independent plan-array entry
findings.details: The live docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json packs[] array begins at CP00-691 (closeout-pack-plan.json:400-402); CP00-690 appears only in live_state (line 36). As a result both the contract validation script (validate-rp22-external-integrations-i-contract.mjs:346-349) and the model test (packages/integrations-core/test/model.test.js:336-338) resolve the CP00-690 plan pack through the `?? cp690Manifest.plan_binding_snapshot` fallback, so the live pack's coverage is checked against a snapshot embedded in the pack's own manifest rather than an independent plan-array entry. Risk is low: the coverage validator (packages/integrations-core/src/validators.js:1023-1041) checks that snapshot against the frozen EXTERNAL_INTEGRATIONS_I_CP690_REQUIREMENTS constants hard-coded in registry.js (deliverable/phase/micro-phase/micro-title counts, independently verified to sum to 150), which serve as the code-reviewed anchor, and the manifest snapshot shares the live plan's source_ledger_sha (13c7ed65...) indicating common generator provenance.
findings.recommendation: No change required for closeout. Optionally, a future generator run could also emit the current/live pack into plan.packs[] (or a dedicated closed-packs array) so the live pack's coverage is cross-validated against a plan-level entry independent of its own manifest.
findings.post_review_disposition: Accepted as expected, low-risk design; non-blocking. Frozen REQUIREMENTS constants in registry.js are the independent coverage anchor, so the verification chain remains sound.
